title: Box truck strikes Parkway Bridge again
summary: A box truck struck the underside of the Parkway Bridge in Salina on August 1, 2025, closing all traffic lanes between Oswego Street and Park Street. The incident occurred two days after Syracuse Mayor Ben Walsh proposed installing "clanker" devices—audible warning systems positioned before bridges to alert drivers of insufficient clearance—following repeated strikes at the Park Street railroad bridge. No injuries were reported.

political_signal.description: Mayor Walsh's recent proposal for clanker devices reflects emerging focus on reducing bridge strikes through low-cost preventive infrastructure; incident timing reinforces urgency of intervention strategy.

processing_notes: Parkway Bridge owned/operated by NYS, not City of Syracuse—intergovernmental coordination required
Article mentions Park Street railroad bridge as separate recurring problem site within City limits
Clanker device proposal represents potential innovation/pilot candidate for CIO review
No budget, contract, or implementation details provided in article
